description: Peer-reviewed scientific basis for V.I.C.T.O.R.'s voice-biomarker triage. Cited by E.L.M.E.R. in evidence reports and referenced in M.E.R.C.E.D.'s clinical glosses.

references.authors: Sara JDS, Maor E, Borlaug B, Lewis BR, Orbelo D, Lerman LO, Lerman A
references.title: Noninvasive Voice Biomarker Is Associated With Incident Coronary Artery Disease Events at Follow-up
key_findings: 6 acoustic features analysed: pitch, jitter, shimmer, MFCCs, loudness, formants
High voice biomarker score predicted ER visits for chest pain, positive stress tests, and confirmed coronary artery blockage
references.short_cite: Sara et al., Mayo Clinic Proceedings 2022

references.authors: Maor E, Perry D, Mevorach D, Taiblum N, Luz Y, Mazin I, Lerman A, Koren G, Shalev V
references.title: Vocal Biomarker Is Associated With Hospitalization and Mortality Among Heart Failure Patients
references.journal: Journal of the American Heart Association
key_findings: Acoustic features: Mel Cepstrum, pitch, jitter, shimmer, loudness
Voice biomarker independently predicted hospitalisation and all-cause mortality

references.authors: Amir O, Abraham WT, Azzam ZS, Berger G, Anker SD, Pinney SP, Burkhoff D, Shallom ID, Lotan C, Edelman ER
references.title: Non-invasive vocal biomarker is associated with pulmonary hypertension
key_findings: Voice analysis indirectly assesses sympathetic nervous system state
Provides a non-invasive means to identify patients at elevated cardiac risk

references.authors: Gómez-Gómez I, Pérez-Marín M, Ferrari-Goelzer L, Hellín-Gil MF, Berenguer A, Cerveró-Sahuquillo S, Rico-Bordera P, Talavera M, Fierro-Marrero J
references.title: The Fundamental Frequency of Voice as a Potential Stress Biomarker: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
key_findings: Stress significantly increases vocal fundamental frequency (F0)
Effect is significantly STRONGER in women
Effect is significantly STRONGER during spontaneous speech (vs read-aloud)
Direct relevance to V.I.C.T.O.R.: kiosk captures spontaneous patient speech, and women are the most at-risk population for atypical-CVD under-triage

guidance.when_to_cite: Cite at least one reference whenever a concordance flag fires AND the biomarker signal is what triggered (or co-triggered) the flag. The Sara/Maor papers ground the cardiovascular link; Gómez-Gómez grounds the women-and-spontaneous-speech amplification, which is V.I.C.T.O.R.'s exact use case.
guidance.format: Inline parenthetical: '(Sara et al., Mayo Clinic Proceedings 2022)'. Do not include URLs in the prose — they appear in the report's References section.
constraints: Do not overstate. These are observational / cohort findings, not clinical-trial-grade causal proof.
Do not cite a paper for a claim the paper does not support — match the `supports` tags to the signal.
Always pair scientific citation with the patient-specific reasoning; don't let the literature do the clinical work.
